title: The Evangelion Breakfast Club
wikitext:
[]: The Evangelion Breakfast Club             Quote Post by Hopelessromantic » Tue Jul 21, 2020 1:09 am Upon searching through the web, a thought entered my mind. What if there was something out there that put Evangelion and The Breakfast Club together? Both address similar themes about the struggles of adolescence and the fact that there truly are others who have went through hardships of their own. Lo and behold, I come across this underrated gem that’s been sitting in YouTube unnoticed for 10 years! Take a gander! Ladies and gentlemen, The Evangelion Breakfast Club: https://youtu.be/JYD3Lvf7_3A

[]: Re: The Evangelion Breakfast Club             Quote Post by C.T.1290 » Tue Jul 21, 2020 4:22 pm I just watched the video. I’ll be honest that I haven’t seen the Breakfast Club before, but it seemed like a pretty popular film from the 80’s. The video was put together rather well. Now I need to make it a point to see the film if I ever get a chance.

[]: Re: The Evangelion Breakfast Club             Quote Post by Gus Hanson » Tue Jul 21, 2020 7:34 pm C.T.1290 wrote: I’ll be honest that I haven’t seen the Breakfast Club before, but it seemed like a pretty popular film from the 80’s. The video was put together rather well. Now I need to make it a point to see the film if I ever get a chance.

[]: Re: The Evangelion Breakfast Club             Quote Post by MirrorUniverseAsuka » Wed Jul 22, 2020 1:27 am >Shinji >a jock That said, it's certainly an interesting thought and if it hasn't been done already in all the AU fanfic that abounds on the internet then I'll be shocked.

[]: Re: The Evangelion Breakfast Club             Quote Post by Hopelessromantic » Wed Jul 29, 2020 6:17 pm Yeah, Shinji if I’m going to be honest is a bit more of a rebel than a jock. That’s really the only problem I have with this video. I wonder, if Rei, Toji, Kaworu, Shinji, and Asuka were all together in a room for eight hours, could they accomplish anything like the Breakfast Club did?

[]: Re: The Evangelion Breakfast Club             Quote Post by Hopelessromantic » Sat Aug 01, 2020 4:45 pm If I could place the characters of Evangelion into the cast of the Breakfast Club, disregarding gender for personality, here is what I’d choose. Toji Suzuhara - The Criminal Asuka Langley Soryu - The Princess Kaworu - The Athlete Rei Ayanami - The Brain Shinji Ikari - The Basket Case Gendo Ikari - The Principal What are your thoughts? Whom would you choose?
